pi_items.metadata_value: Hill, Griffiths, Lim - Principles of Econometrics 5th ed, household survey data
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic proxy seeded with 5 real observations
pi_items.source: Cross-referenced data points in install_gretl.sh against the true food.dat from POE website. Found that only the first 5 observations are real; the remaining 35 were hallucinated synthetically, resulting in a significantly different distribution.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires running White's heteroskedasticity test on the food.gdt dataset in Gretl. The agent must regress 'food_exp' on 'income', perform the White test from the OLS results window, and save the output exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/white_test_results.txt'. While the dataset claims to be real household survey data from Principles of Econometrics 5th ed., analysis of the embedded data in install_gretl.sh reveals that only the first 5 observations are real; the other 35 were synthetically hallucinated, resulting in an artificially high R-squared of 0.88 compared to the real dataset's 0.38.
